title: Determination of the sensitivity of the DEAP-3600 experiment to supermassive charged gravitinos
abstract: The lack of discovery of particle dark matter candidates within the favored mass-window range brings in the motivation for the study of new options brought by Planck-mass dark matter models. Extended supergravity theories predict the existence of non-relativistic gravitinos that could at least in part contribute to the missing mass-energy density of the Universe. The feasibility study for the discovery with DEAP-3600 experiment of Planck mass charged gravitino dark matter is presented. Additionally the expected signal events topology within the detector is discussed.
comment: 19th International Conference on Topics in Astroparticle and Underground Physics (TAUP2025), conference processing
